title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: The local council has asked residents to submit a report on the condition of public transport in your town. Your report should describe the current situation, identify the main problems, and recommend improvements that the council should prioritise.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on the Condition of Public Transport in Millford

Introduction
This report has been prepared in response to the council's public consultation on transport services. It outlines the current state of public transport in Millford, identifies the most pressing issues, and sets out a series of recommendations for improvement.

Current Situation
Millford is served by three bus routes and a single rail connection to the city centre. While these services are theoretically adequate for a town of this size, their reliability has declined markedly over the past two years. Buses frequently arrive late or not at all, and the rail service operates at severely reduced capacity during off-peak hours.

Main Problems
The principal concerns raised by residents can be summarised as follows:
- Chronic unreliability and unpredictable timetabling, particularly on the No. 7 and No. 12 bus routes.
- Insufficient frequency of evening services, forcing residents to rely on private transport or taxis.
- Poor accessibility at three out of four bus stops on the northern circuit, which lack shelter or seating.

Recommendations
The council should prioritise the following actions:
- Introducing a real-time tracking application to provide passengers with accurate arrival information.
- Extending bus services to 11 pm on weekdays and midnight on weekends.
- Upgrading infrastructure at the most-used stops to include weatherproof shelters and seating.

Conclusion
Addressing these issues would significantly improve the quality of life for residents and reduce the town's dependence on private vehicles.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement for a summer internship at an international publishing house. Write an email to the HR department expressing your interest in the position, explaining why you are a suitable candidate, and asking for further details about the application process.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear HR Department,

I am writing to express my strong interest in the summer internship position advertised on your company's website last week. Having studied English Literature and Creative Writing at university for the past two years, I believe I have both the academic background and the personal enthusiasm to make a meaningful contribution to your team.

Throughout my studies, I have developed a keen editorial eye and a thorough understanding of narrative structure across a range of genres. I have also gained practical experience by volunteering as a sub-editor for our university's literary magazine, where I was responsible for reviewing and revising submitted manuscripts to publication standard. I am highly organised, deadline-driven, and work equally well both independently and as part of a collaborative team.

I am particularly drawn to your publishing house because of your renowned commitment to promoting debut authors. The opportunity to work within an environment that values literary discovery aligns perfectly with my own professional ambitions, and I am eager to learn from the expertise of your editorial staff.

Could you please let me know the deadline for applications, whether the internship offers any financial remuneration, and if there is a specific portfolio or writing sample you would like applicants to submit alongside their CV?

I would be grateful for any further information and look forward to hearing from you at your earliest convenience.

Yours faithfully,
Naomi Griffiths

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen the following advertisement in an international travel magazine: 'We are collecting reviews of city breaks that exceeded all expectations. Tell us where you went, what made the experience special, and whether you would return.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Beyond Expectations: A Weekend in Porto

I arrived in Porto with modest expectations and left four days later having experienced one of the most captivating cities in Europe. What began as a simple city break became an unexpectedly profound cultural immersion.

The city's physical beauty alone is worth the journey. Porto's famous azulejo tile facades cascade down vertiginous hillsides to the banks of the Douro River, creating a visual landscape unlike anything I had encountered before. The atmospheric, labyrinthine streets of the Ribeira district invite aimless exploration, and every corner seems to reveal a new café, bookshop, or hidden courtyard. The Livraria Lello, widely regarded as one of the most beautiful bookshops in the world, fully lives up to its reputation.

What truly distinguished Porto, however, was the warmth and sincerity of its residents. Unlike some heavily touristed cities where locals have grown weary of visitors, the people of Porto seemed genuinely proud to share their city and its traditions. Conversations over a glass of local wine at a tascas restaurant turned into hour-long exchanges about history, football, and food.

Food, of course, is central to any Porto experience. The bacalhau dishes are exceptional, and the iconic pastel de nata served at the oldest patisseries in the city is a sensory revelation.

I would return to Porto without hesitation. It is a city that reveals itself gradually, generously, and in ways that linger long after the journey home.
